goal: Investigate and fix log --sync showing merged intents as proposed

summary.title: Force explicit log --sync to refresh despite freshness window
summary.what: Changed the CLI auto-sync wrapper so explicit `mainline log --sync` bypasses the configured freshness window, while implicit auto-sync commands such as `status` and `check` remain freshness-gated. Added focused CLI tests for the explicit log path and the implicit status path.
summary.why: A freshly merged PR could still appear as `[proposed]` when `mainline log --sync` was run inside the 5-minute freshness window. The flag was routed through the shared auto-sync wrapper, and the wrapper treated the explicit request the same as implicit auto-sync, so it could skip `Service.Sync()` and read the stale local view. A later direct sync pinned the same intents correctly, proving the issue was the freshness gate, not the pin matching logic.
summary.user_goal: Explain why merged intents were still showing as proposed and fix the `log --sync` behavior so it really refreshes.

decisions.rationale: The user explicitly asked for fresh team data. Reusing the freshness cache made the flag misleading and allowed post-merge proposed statuses to persist until another true sync ran. This directly follows the prior auto-sync intent int_01cb031f, which kept log out of implicit auto-sync because it already had an opt-in freshness flag.
Those commands auto-sync without a per-command explicit request, so the existing cost-control decision from int_01cb031f still applies. The fix is scoped to explicit log sync semantics.

The existing sync/auto-pin design from int_dee8ff6f worked once a real sync ran; commit notes were written with `via=pin_auto` and the intents became merged. The bug was that `log --sync` could avoid calling sync at all.

rejected.alternative: Remove the freshness window for all auto-syncing commands.
rejected.reason: That would undo the deliberate cost-control behavior for implicit commands and is broader than the observed bug.

rejected.alternative: Special-case log output to reclassify proposed intents without syncing.
rejected.reason: Merged status is derived from synced notes and the rebuilt view. Reclassifying at presentation time would duplicate engine logic and still miss fresh remote actor-log or note data.

architectural_claims: Explicit per-command sync requests bypass freshness gating.
Implicit auto-sync commands remain freshness-gated through autoSyncCommands.
behavioral_changes: `mainline log --sync` now calls sync even when LastSync is inside Sync.FreshnessSeconds.
`mainline status` and `mainline check` keep the existing freshness-window behavior.
